sections.heading: Clinical Follow-up Schedule
sections.content: For patients with no evidence of clinical disease: clinical follow-up with imaging every 6–12 months for up to 5 years.12 For patients with active disease or in remission: follow-up frequency determined by clinical need.12

sections.heading: Imaging Strategy
sections.content: Initial evaluation: contrast-enhanced chest/abdomen/pelvis CT. FDG-PET/CT is an alternative only if contrast is contraindicated.85 Follow-up: imaging as indicated by symptoms and clinical status. Consider FDG-PET/CT in select situations, especially when considering local or regional therapy.85

sections.heading: Laboratory Monitoring
sections.content: Initial: complete blood count (CBC), comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) as indicated.1 Disease-specific markers as indicated (e.g., PSA for prostate, CA-125 for ovarian, AFP for liver).1 Follow-up labs as clinically indicated.

sections.heading: Supportive Follow-up
sections.content: Psychosocial support and distress management per NCCN Guidelines for Distress Management. Palliative care and symptom management per NCCN Guidelines for Palliative Care and Adult Cancer Pain. Survivorship care per NCCN Guidelines for Survivorship.

timeline.title: Initial Evaluation
timeline.body: Contrast-enhanced chest/abdomen/pelvis CT. FDG-PET/CT if contrast contraindicated. Complete blood count (CBC), comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH), and disease-specific markers as indicated.

timeline.title: Every 6–12 Months (up to 5 Years)
timeline.body: For patients with no evidence of clinical disease: clinical follow-up with imaging every 6–12 months. Imaging as indicated by symptoms and clinical status. Consider FDG-PET/CT in select situations. Follow-up labs as clinically indicated.

timeline.title: After 5 Years or As Needed
timeline.body: For patients with active disease or in remission: follow-up frequency determined by clinical need. Imaging and labs as indicated by symptoms and clinical status. Supportive follow-up including psychosocial support, palliative care, and survivorship care per NCCN Guidelines.
